prompt: Add a password strength validator to our auth module. It should check minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, and one special character. Return a score from 0-4 based on how many criteria are met. The file is at src/auth/validators.ts and tests go in tests/auth/validators.test.ts.
expectations: Test written BEFORE implementation code — a test file exists before the validator function is implemented
Test is run and verified to fail before any implementation is written
The initial failure is for the right reason (missing function/module, not a syntax error)
Minimal implementation written to make failing tests pass — no over-engineering beyond what tests require
Tests run again and verified to pass after implementation
At least 3 separate red-green cycles visible (not one big batch of tests then one big implementation)
Final implementation correctly returns score 0 for empty string
Final implementation correctly returns score 4 for a password meeting all criteria
Tests cover all 5 criteria: min length, uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character
